NUTRITION_DIARY_PAGE.TITLE: Online Food Diary for Meals, Calories, Macros, and Progress
NUTRITION_DIARY_PAGE.SUBTITLE: Food Diary helps you keep a food diary without separating logs, goals, and results: add meals, track calories and macros, plan nutrition, and review weight, waist, and habit trends.

PANEL.TITLE: A food diary is more useful when it connects planning and analytics
PANEL.TEXT: Food Diary is built for people who want more than a static food log: meals, calories, macros, recipes, plans, weight, waist, weekly review, and dietologist collaboration stay connected.

ITEMS.MEALS: Log breakfasts, lunches, dinners, and snacks
ITEMS.CALORIES: Track calories, macros, and nutrition totals
ITEMS.PLANS: Plan meals and build shopping lists
ITEMS.BODY: Review weight, waist, and weekly progress
ITEMS.FIRST: Log breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks
ITEMS.SECOND: Review calories, macros, and daily totals
ITEMS.THIRD: Plan nutrition and build shopping lists
ITEMS.FOURTH: Compare meals with weight, waist, and trends

FEATURES.TITLE: What an online food diary should cover
FEATURES.SUBTITLE: This page is focused on the core jobs users expect from a modern food diary app and the extra workflows that help Food Diary stand out.

MEAL_LOGGING.TITLE: Log meals without rebuilding the same entries
MEAL_LOGGING.TEXT: Track food with reusable products, recipes, and meal records so daily logging stays fast after the first week.

CALORIES.TITLE: See calories and macros as you log
CALORIES.TEXT: Calories, proteins, fats, and carbs stay visible while you build your day instead of appearing only after the meal is saved.

PLANNING.TITLE: Plan meals before hunger makes the decision
PLANNING.TEXT: Use meal plans and shopping lists to move from reactive logging toward a clearer nutrition routine.

PROGRESS.TITLE: Review long-term nutrition and body trends
PROGRESS.TEXT: Charts, weekly check-ins, weight history, and waist tracking help you compare routine changes with actual progress.

DIETOLOGIST.TITLE: Keep dietologist collaboration inside the app
DIETOLOGIST.TEXT: Invite a dietologist by email and share only the data categories you want visible instead of sending manual reports.

FASTING.TITLE: Add focused workflows such as fasting
FASTING.TEXT: Food Diary can support more structured health routines when you need more than a basic meal diary.

STEPS.TITLE: Start using the food diary in three steps
STEPS.SUBTITLE: The core workflow is simple enough for daily use but still supports more advanced planning and review.

ACCOUNT.TITLE: Create your account
ACCOUNT.TEXT: Set up your profile so your diary, goals, and progress stay synchronized over time.

LOG.TITLE: Log meals and build your day
LOG.TEXT: Add products, recipes, and portions while keeping nutrition totals visible across the day.

REVIEW.TITLE: Review trends and adjust your routine
REVIEW.TEXT: Use weekly summaries, body metrics, and nutrition charts to see what is actually working.

FAQ.TITLE: Common questions about using Food Diary
FAQ.SUBTITLE: These answers focus on the most common expectations behind queries such as food diary, online nutrition diary, and calorie diary.

APP_SCOPE.QUESTION: How is Food Diary different from a basic food log?
APP_SCOPE.ANSWER: Food Diary keeps meals, calories, macros, recipes, meal plans, weight, waist, fasting, and weekly review in one connected context.

CALORIES.QUESTION: Can I use it as a calorie and macro tracker?
CALORIES.ANSWER: Yes. Calories, protein, fat, and carbs stay visible across the day and remain connected to meals, foods, and recipes.

PLANNING.QUESTION: Can I plan meals in advance in the same app?
PLANNING.ANSWER: Yes. Food Diary supports meal plans, recipes, and shopping lists so future days are easier to prepare.

DIETOLOGIST.QUESTION: Can I keep a food diary with a dietologist?
DIETOLOGIST.ANSWER: Yes. You can invite a dietologist and share only selected data categories, then change access later if needed.

CTA.TITLE: Start with a food diary and grow into a full nutrition workflow
CTA.SUBTITLE: Create an account to log meals, track calories and macros, plan nutrition, and keep your progress in one connected system.

AUDIENCE.SUBTITLE: This page helps explain when Food Diary is more useful than a simple note app or standalone calorie counter.

FOURTH.TEXT: Selected data can be shared with a dietologist without manual reports.

REPEAT_MEALS.TEXT: Repeatable breakfasts, lunches, and prepped meals reduce manual work.

GOALS.TITLE: Keep nutrition goals nearby

ADJUSTMENTS.TITLE: Adjust without losing structure

FOLLOW.TITLE: Follow and refine

FAQ.TITLE: Common questions about meal prep in Food Diary
FAQ.SUBTITLE: Answers for people searching for a meal prep planner, weekly menu, or meal prep with shopping lists.

PREP.QUESTION: Can I use Food Diary for meal prep?
PREP.ANSWER: Yes. Food Diary helps you plan meals ahead, reuse recipes, and connect prep with shopping.

SHOPPING.QUESTION: Can I make a shopping list for meal prep?
SHOPPING.ANSWER: Yes. Planning can connect with shopping lists so groceries are easier to prepare in advance.

RECIPES.QUESTION: Can I repeat the same recipes?
RECIPES.ANSWER: Yes. Saved recipes work well as repeatable meal prep blocks.

FLEXIBLE.QUESTION: Can I change the plan during the week?
FLEXIBLE.ANSWER: Yes. The plan can be adjusted while keeping meals, shopping, and nutrition goals organized.
